channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Monitoring AI Agent Actions in Production: A Developer's Guide
item.description: Monitoring AI Agent Actions in Production: A Developer's Guide You deploy an AI agent to production. It's supposed to fill out forms, make API calls, and report back. For the first week,...

item.title: Add Authentication to Next.js With One Line of Code
item.description: export default authMiddleware() That’s it. One line in your middleware file and your Next.js app has authentication — sign-up, sign-in, sign-out, session management, and protected routes. No...

item.title: Why good talks don’t get selected
item.description: There’s something I’ve been thinking about for a long time, especially after applying to talks, getting selected, getting rejected, speaking at conferences, and also helping organize community...

item.title: OpenVoiceUI -AI-Voice Agent App Generates Live Canvas Pages Using OpenClaw
item.description: If you've been following OpenClaw — the open-source AI gateway that routes to any LLM provider — you've probably wondered: what can I actually build on top of it? OpenVoiceUI is the first voice UI...

item.title: How I Structure My AI Agent Workspace (And Why It Matters)
item.description: How I Structure My AI Agent Workspace (And Why It Matters) I've spent the last few months building and running AI agents — everything from research bots to content generators to operational...

item.title: Want the Answer Engines to Send You Traffic? Show Up in Organic
item.description: Editorial note: I originally wrote this over on the Hit Subscribe blog. When it comes to answering the question, "what causes a site to earn more answer engine traffic," I have two disparate...

item.title: Why Garry Tan’s Claude Code setup has gotten so much love, and hate
item.description: Thousands of people are trying Garry Tan's Claude Code setup, which was shared on Github. And everyone has an opinion: even Claude, ChatGPT, and Gemini.

item.title: AWS Transit Gateway vs Azure Virtual WAN vs GCP NCC: A Side-by-Side for Network Engineers Going Multi-Cloud
item.description: Every cloud provider has its own hub-and-spoke networking service, and they are not interchangeable. If you're a network engineer moving into multi-cloud — or already juggling VPCs across AWS,...

item.title: I went to the Pentagon to watch Pete Hegseth scold war reporters
item.description: It is day 13 of America's surprise war with Iran - by sheer coincidence, it's Friday the 13th - and I am delirious. I haven't had a coffee since I woke up at 5AM, because I'm not allowed to bring...

item.title: What problems do you face with queue systems?
item.description: I’m currently building QueueForge (https://queueforge.dev) and I’d love to learn from people who work with queue-based systems. If you use message queues in production, what are the biggest...

item.title: QueueForge Website Launch
item.description: I have just published the new marketing website for QueueForge. (https://queueforge.dev) QueueForge is a project I am currently building around observability and tooling for queue based systems....

item.title: I built 116 AI tools into one API — and added crypto payments
item.description: For the past few months I've been building AI agents that need to call a lot of different APIs: web search, news, weather, stock prices, crypto data, email validation, image generation... Every...

item.title: The Beats Studio Pro are nearly $200 off ahead of Amazon’s big spring sale
item.description: Earlier this week, Apple unveiled the AirPods Max 2. The forthcoming over-ear headphones build on their predecessor with an H2 chip that powers a range of new features; however, they still don’t...

item.title: Remedy’s live-service shooter Firebreak is getting its final major update
item.description: Remedy is winding down its team shooter FBC: Firebreak with a big update that launches today. But while the game won't be getting any new content going forward, the studio intends to keep it alive...

item.title: Spotify adds ‘Exclusive Mode’ audiophile feature for Windows PCs
item.description: Starting Tuesday, Spotify users will have access to "Exclusive Mode," a new feature in the streaming service's Windows app that can help maximize audio quality by giving the Spotify app full...

item.title: Ikea tried to build a smart home for everyone — here’s why it’s not working yet
item.description: Ikea's new Matter-over-Thread products were supposed to prove that the smart home could be cheap, accessible, and reliable. The highly anticipated line - which includes sensors, remotes, smart...

item.title: The Pentagon is developing alternatives to Anthropic, report says
item.description: After their dramatic falling-out, it doesn't seem as though Anthropic and the Pentagon are getting back together.

item.title: Nvidia’s DLSS 5 is like motion smoothing for video games, but worse
item.description: Yesterday Nvidia revealed its latest upscaling tech, called DLSS 5, which it described as "the company's most significant breakthrough in computer graphics since the debut of real-time ray tracing...

item.title: Antigravity’s 360-degree drone is 20 percent off ahead of its next feature drop
item.description: Insta360’s Antigravity A1 is one of the most interesting drones on the market thanks to its included Vision Goggles, which provide a 360-degree first-person view through the drone’s cameras (a...

item.title: BuzzFeed debuts AI slop apps in bid for new revenue
item.description: BuzzFeed unveiled new AI-powered social apps at SXSW, but its demos drew muted reactions.

item.title: Researchers disclose vulnerabilities in IP KVMs from four manufacturers
item.description: Internet-exposed devices that give BIOS-level access? What could possibly go wrong?

item.title: The messiah rises in Dune: Part Three’s new trailer
item.description: First comes love, then comes marriage, then comes a portentous set of twin babies in the new trailer for Dune: Part Three. Warner Bros. has debuted its first full trailer for Denis Villeneuve's...

item.title: AI Flaws in Amazon Bedrock, LangSmith, and SGLang Enable Data Exfiltration and RCE
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a new method for exfiltrating sensitive data from artificial intelligence (AI) code execution environments using domain name system (DNS)...

item.title: State of Open Source on Hugging Face: Spring 2026
Now everyone in the US is getting Google’s personalized Gemini AI
item.description: Google announced on Tuesday that all users in the US will now have access to its Personal Intelligence feature, which lets you connect various Google apps to provide context for Gemini's responses...

item.title: Google’s Personal Intelligence feature is expanding to all US users
item.description: Personal Intelligence allows Google's AI assistant to tap into your Google ecosystem, such as Gmail and Google Photos, to provide more tailored responses.

item.title: Bringing the power of Personal Intelligence to more people
item.description: Bubble that says "Personal Intelligence" with Google G, Google Photos logo, and Gmail logo around it

item.title: Our latest investment in open source security for the AI era
item.description: A collage including security icons and photos of hands clasped, a man looking at a computer, and two people pointing at something off camera

item.title: OpenAI expands government footprint with AWS deal, report says
item.description: OpenAI has reportedly signed a partnership with AWS to sell its AI systems to the U.S. government for classified and unclassified work, marking an expansion beyond its Pentagon deal last month.

item.title: AI’s ‘boys’ club’ could widen the wealth gap for women, says Rana el Kaliouby
item.description: AI investor Rana el Kaliouby warns that if women are shut out of AI funding and leadership, the consequences will be grim.
